articles.title: A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria
articles.description: Haven’t you heard? White-collar jobs are going away, decimated by AI. Waves of layoffs in the tech sector (most recently at Coinbase and Meta and Cisco) are said to presage what will soon come for all of us knowledge workers. But before you quit your job as a software developer or financial analyst—
articles.content: Haven’t you heard? White-collar jobs are going away, decimated by AI. Waves of layoffs in the tech sector (most recently at Coinbase and Meta and Cisco) are said to presage what will soon come for all of us knowledge workers. But before you quit your job as a software developer or financial analyst—or tech journalist—and look to join the plumbers’ union, it’s worth considering today’s economic research on whether artificial intelligence has actually begun to devour white-collar work. The short a
articles.author: David Rotman

articles.title: The Download: puncturing the AI jobs panic
articles.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria Despite the growing hysteria over AI’s threat to white-collar jobs, there’s still scant evidence that the tech
articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria Despite the growing hysteria over AI’s threat to white-collar jobs, there’s still scant evidence that the technology has had a large-scale impact on the labor market. Analysis of US labor data shows that unemployment in occupations most exposed to AI is actually lower than in less-exposed jobs. There are als
articles.author: Thomas Macaulay

articles.description: CBS and Paramount backed away from copyright challenges to limit distribution of Stephen Colbert's appearance on a Michigan cable access show. He ended his run as host of "The Late Show" on Thursday.

articles.content: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health , as previously announced , and many of the responses we've seen so far are full of confusion, frustration, and requests to get the old app back. One post on Reddit calls out a common issue, saying, "I can't even completely fill up my home screen. They only have 2 large tiles available and I can't j
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
articles.description: "We also obviously want to be very mindful of the Outer Space Treaty."
articles.content: NASA officials announced contract awards for the initial elements of a lunar base on Tuesday, including two rovers that will provide mobility to astronauts. With the series of announcements, NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman sought to maintain momentum around a Moon Base initiative revealed two months ago as part of the space agency's return to the Moon. "For those waiting patiently, the grand return is close at hand, and we will not slow down," he said. The manager for the lunar base, Carlos Ga
articles.author: Eric Berger

articles.title: Do you have what it takes to run AI in production?
articles.description: From the floor of HumanX, Ryan Donovan is joined by Peter Salanki, CTO and co-founder of CoreWeave, to chat about what it really takes to run AI in production; the growing importance of observability, utilization, and scheduling; and Peter’s advice for avoiding the trap of over-architecting too earl
articles.content: From the floor of HumanX, Ryan Donovan is joined by Peter Salanki, CTO and co-founder of CoreWeave, to chat about what it really takes to run AI in production; the growing importance of observability, utilization, and scheduling; and Peter’s advice for avoiding the trap of over-architecting too early.

articles.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
articles.description: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Base missions NASA says are "the first of more than a dozen missions that will be announced this year.
articles.content: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Base missions NASA says are "the first of more than a dozen missions that will be announced this year." Moon Base I, launching "no earlier than fall 2026," will use Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 1 Endurance lander to bring NASA payloads to the Moon, including the Stereo Camera for Lunar Plume-Surface S
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: We're starting to see some PC makers respond to Apple's MacBook Neo
articles.description: Sub-$600 laptops have existed for years, but consistently good ones remain rare.
articles.content: It seems fair to say that Apple's MacBook Neo took the rest of the PC industry by surprise. Companies are used to competing on price and features with $1,000-and-up Apple laptops like the MacBook Air and MacBook Pro, but their $600 and $700 models usually come with cut corners and compromises that are more noticeable than the Neo's. The CEO of Asus admitted to being surprised by the laptop's price (while simultaneously trying to downplay the Neo's value); a Microsoft-backed study comparing PCs t
articles.author: Andrew Cunningham

articles.title: I Built a Free Finance Dashboard as a Solo Dev — Here's What I Learned
articles.description: I was tired of using 5 different apps to track my money. One for budgeting, one for crypto prices, one for dividends, one for market news. So I built my own. It's called CollectCX — a free finance dashboard that tracks everything in one place. No signup, no account, your data stays in your browser.
articles.content: I was tired of using 5 different apps to track my money. One for budgeting, one for crypto prices, one for dividends, one for market news. So I built my own. It's called CollectCX — a free finance dashboard that tracks everything in one place. No signup, no account, your data stays in your browser. Here's what it does: Year-at-a-glance budget spreadsheet for tracking income and expenses Live crypto prices updated in real time Dividend calendar with ex-dates for 27+ stocks Market news feed coveri
articles.author: CollectCX

articles.title: Drive JHipster with your AI agent: introducing jhipster-mcp (v0.0.4)
articles.description: TL;DR — jhipster-mcp is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that lets an AI agent generate and evolve JHipster applications for you. You describe what you want in plain language; the agent writes JDL and drives the JHipster CLI. It's on npm — point your MCP host at it with one line. v0.0.4
articles.content: TL;DR — jhipster-mcp is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that lets an AI agent generate and evolve JHipster applications for you. You describe what you want in plain language; the agent writes JDL and drives the JHipster CLI. It's on npm — point your MCP host at it with one line. v0.0.4 is the first public release. 📦 npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/jhipster-mcp 🧑‍💻 GitHub: https://github.com/avdev4j/jhipster-mcp The idea JHipster has always been about speed : scaffold a product
articles.author: Anthony Viard

articles.content: Welcome back to GitHub for Beginners. We’ve covered a lot this season, so make sure to check out our other episodes. This time, we’re going to take a look at VS Code, a free popular source code editor provided by Microsoft. Into the Spider-Verse's sumptuous visuals and focus on a
articles.content: After years of it seeming like the Spider-Man film rights might be better off in Marvel's hands alone, Into the Spider-Verse came along and proved that Sony was still capable of telling phenomenal stories featuring everyone's favorite webhead. Into the Spider-Verse 's sumptuous visuals and focus on a different web-slinging New Yorker made it unlike any other Spider-Man adaptation. And it was genuinely shocking to see Sony follow the film up with a bigger, bolder, more imaginative sequel just a f

articles.title: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
articles.description: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution.  Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can’t support that change. They
articles.content: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution. Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can’t support that change. They cite a lack of readiness across people, processes, and workflows. The sticky tape problem The challenge is that many organisations are often layering AI agents onto existing operations, rather than reimagin
articles.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

articles.title: Is Peter Thiel the target of Pope Leo's Gandalf quote? An investigation.
articles.description: Parsing a papal proclamation.
articles.content: I'm not suggesting that a man like Pope Leo—the Vicar of Christ, the Bishop of Rome, the Servant of the Servants of God—would stoop to anything quite so base as "trolling" the onetime PayPal co-founder and current Antichrist alarmist Peter Thiel. But I'm also not not suggesting it, if you see what I mean. How else to explain the novel appearance of Gandalf—yes, the pipe-smoking wizard!—in the pages of one of Catholicism's most important documents, a major papal encyclical about AI and technology
articles.author: Nate Anderson

articles.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
articles.description: Musk says drones used Starlink instead of Starshield, blames military contractor.
articles.content: SpaceX and the Pentagon have been bickering about the price of using Starshield satellite service during the Iran war, according to a Reuters report published today. It appears that SpaceX asked the military for more money after it started using satellite terminals on "kamikaze" attack drones in Iran. SpaceX CEO Elon Musk claimed the Reuters report is wrong. But Musk also said the military drones initially used the commercial Starlink service instead of the government-specific network, in violat
articles.author: Jon Brodkin
